claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, performance, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: React Aria implements the APG listbox pattern over a labelled role=listbox and role=option descendants; sections expose labelled groups.
semantics.naming: A required persistent visible label owns aria-labelledby. Descriptions, errors, and a bounded selected-text summary use stable references.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides, interactive option descendants, and alternate focus engines are outside the supported contract.

interaction.keyboard: Tab enters one composite stop; Arrow Up/Down move direct option focus; Home/End move to boundaries; printable characters use locale-aware typeahead; Space selects; multiple mode uses toggle behavior without timing-dependent chords.
interaction.pointer: Selection commits on React Aria press completion rather than pointer down; disabled items do not activate.
interaction.touch: Options and retry/load-more actions preserve preferred 44 CSS pixel targets and require no path gesture.
interaction.speech: The visible listbox label, option textValue, and explicit retry/load-more button labels remain voice target names.
interaction.switchAccess: One composite and ordinary recovery buttons remain reachable without hidden duplicate option controls.

formBehavior: A named selection serializes each canonical key through hidden successful controls, producing one value in single mode and repeated same-name values in multiple mode. Disabled omits values. Dynamic page replacement retains a bounded copy of selected records and initial reset defaults, so entry churn cannot silently erase FormData or fabricate a change callback. Uncontrolled form reset restores the initial default unless canceled; controlled state remains parent-owned. aria-required requires application validation because no false native constraint proxy is introduced.

states.synchronization: Visible checks, aria-selected, callback values, hidden form values, selected summaries, retained selected records, and controlled/uncontrolled ownership derive from the same ordered key set.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Selected state combines aria-selected, a check, weight, and boundary geometry rather than color alone.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses Canvas, CanvasText, Highlight, and visible system-color focus outlines while retaining the check cue.
preferences.reducedMotion: No selection or loading information depends on motion; scrolling uses immediate browser behavior under reduced motion.

responsive.overflowPolicy: The collection scrolls in the block axis, long labels wrap, controls stack in narrow containers, and the page does not gain two-dimensional overflow.

internationalization.imePolicy: Listbox has no editable text surface; printable-key handling is delegated to locale-aware React Aria typeahead and does not intercept active text composition elsewhere.
internationalization.serialization: Labels and summaries are localized; key serialization remains canonical consumer-owned machine data.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide stable keys that do not change when items reorder, filter, paginate, or localize.
Keep keys stable across pages and explicitly clear or replace selections when records are no longer valid; Listbox retains observed selected records when a page merely omits them.
Provide localized textValue for every item and section, including rich-rendered options.
Validate required Listbox selection in the owning workflow and associate persistent error text.
Use useCollectionLoader or an equivalently abortable, stale-safe adapter for remote data.
Do not treat automated axe and browser evidence as manual assistive-technology evidence.
limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, performance baselines, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
Listbox required state is an ARIA and application-validation contract; custom listboxes cannot provide native constraint validation without a misleading proxy.
Selection summaries are bounded to twelve visible names plus an omitted count so large multiple selections do not flood accessibility output.
